Your attacks are just as dangerous if they miss, or even after hitting their mark, as others' are while they're still in the air.

subclassFeature.name: Light-Footed
entries: By 7th level, you're just as good at escaping from trouble as you are at getting into it. Difficult terrain costs you no extra movement.

subclassFeature.name: Deft Deflectionist
entries: Starting at 10th level, as a reaction when an attacker you can see makes a ranged weapon attack against you, you can make an attack with a firearm you are holding to attempt to deflect the projectile. If your attack roll is higher than the attacker's, the attack targeting you has disadvantage. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attacker's attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Pinball Shot
entries: Starting at 14th level, once per turn when you hit a creature with a ranged firearm attack, you can ricochet the bullet to hit an additional target. The second target must be within half the firearm's range of the first target. Make a separate attack roll for the second target. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (a minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Lay Down The Law
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use a bonus action and expend one risk die to keep a keen eye out for dangers that threaten your companions. When you do, roll the risk die and gain a pool of temporary hit points equal to the number rolled + your level in this class. This pool of temporary hit points is immediately divided as you choose among any number of creatures that you can see (other than you) within range of a firearm you are holding. You must give at least 1 temporary hit point to each chosen creature.

When a creature loses temporary hit points granted by this feature as the result of an attack, you can use your reaction to make one ranged weapon attack with a firearm against the attacker. Any remaining temporary hit points granted by this feature are lost at the start of your next turn.
At 10th level, this feature improves: your pool of temporary hit points equals the number rolled on the risk die + twice your level in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Steely-Eyed Aura
entries: Also at 3rd level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened while you are conscious.

subclassFeature.name: White Hat
entries: Some gunslingers live by a code and expect others to do the same. These gunslingers, known as white hats, sometimes serve as agents of local or regional law but never hesitate to do what's right no matter what the rules say. Despite their affinity for deadly weapons, white hats prefer to keep their friends safe and take their enemies in unharmed—a preference they find their enemies aren't often inclined to oblige.

subclassFeature.name: Reach For The Skies
entries: At 7th level, you can use your action to call for your enemy's surrender. When you do, a creature of your choice within 30 feet who can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). Creatures that are immune to being frightened automatically succeed on this saving throw. If the creature's current hit points are equal to or greater than half of its hit point maximum, it has advantage on this saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature is incapacitated and its speed is reduced to 0 for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending this effect on a success.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Long Arm of The Law
entries: At 10th level, once per turn when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a firearm attack, you can choose to deal half as much damage to hobble the target. The creature can't move on its next turn unless it first takes the Disengage action.

subclassFeature.name: Gold Star Hero
entries: At 14th level, your capacity for heroism increases. Your features from this archetype gain the following benefits:

items: While a creature has temporary hit points granted by your Lay Down the Law feature, it has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.
The range of your Steely-Eyed Aura feature increases to 30 feet.
You regain the use of your Reach for the Skies feature when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Conspiracy Theorist
entries: The world is full of stuff that doesn't add up. A natural disaster strikes and they talk about theoretical weather patterns. People forget days at a time and they say some medical jargon about their brains. And nobody seems to notice the folks dressed in black who seem to be lurking behind every corner. It's one big coincidence after another. But when you ask the right questions and pull on the most innocuous threads, you start uncovering the biggest mysteries: the ones They don't want you knowing about.

It's hard to tell who They even are, apart from the fact They're massively influential and have deep pockets. They have agents everywhere, watching and waiting. What are their goals? What are they working so hard to keep secret? You can't be totally sure, but you have a few good theories.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of magical trinkets to help you uncover mysteries that They don't want you to oust. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Masonic Charm
entries: As a bonus action, you can attach this charm to a weapon you are holding. When you do so, pick a number from 2 to 19. For the next minute, the weapon scores a critical hit on attack rolls when you roll that number on a {@dice d20}, in addition to a 20.

entries.name: Three-Headed Coin
entries: As a bonus action, you can reroll one of your attack rolls or ability checks, or as a reaction you can reroll a saving throw you make. You choose which of the d20s is used for the roll.

entries.name: Unfathomable Metal
entries: As a bonus action, you reveal the metal to a creature within 5 feet of you. The target creature takes {@damage 1d6} radiant damage and must succeed a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 1d6} radiant damage at the start of each of its turns for up to 1 minute. The creature can reattempt the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Paranoid Insight
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you have advantage on initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Prepper
entries: Starting at 6th level, your manic predictions of the future pay off in conventionally dangerous situations. Whenever you ready an attack, you have advantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Probably Aliens
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can predict never- before-witnessed phenomena. Any time the GM would disallow other characters to make an Intelligence check to understand something because the knowledge in question has no precedent, is too obscure, or is simply unsupported by the available information, they can allow you to make an Intelligence check to understand it anyway.

subclassFeature.name: Off the Grid
entries: By 14th level, you're so adept at avoiding people that you can always keep them at arm's length. Whenever a hostile creature enters your reach, you can use your reaction to move up to half your movement speed without provoking opportunity attacks. Additionally, you are always under the effects of the {@spell nondetection} spell.

subclassFeature.name: Black Suit
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you can invoke a certain unspoken authority invested in your black suit. You gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill and add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.

subclassFeature.name: Containment Specialist
entries: Though the study of magic is diverse and wonderous, it contains innumerable mysteries better left unexplored. Such topics in magic are seldom studied, for their very existence poses an existential threat to the multiverse itself: chronomancy, protomancy, and quantamancy are among the few graced with names at all. When a sinister (or merely curious) arcanist pulls on one of these volatile threads of knowledge, they open a Pandora's box of unpredictable effects; the results are usually catastrophic. Using such dangerous arcana leaves lasting scars on people and objects, metaphysical reverberations from the terrible secrets at their source.

As their title implies, containment specialists are tasked with tracking, isolating, and containing exotic magic and its artifacts. Their job is hazardous and challenging, but is of critical importance to the multiverse at large. Containment specialists usually coordinate in clandestine groups to triangulate new threats and ensure isolated ones are contained indefinitely. With some luck, these organizations can remain obscure footnotes for generations, further sheltering their secrets from curious eyes.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a handful of safe magical items which aid in your search and containment of far more dangerous artifacts. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Antibell
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the {@spell silence} spell once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Black Bag
entries: As a bonus action, you can speak a secret command word to open your black bag, an item linked to numerous extradimensional spaces. Once the command word is spoken, you can place items in the bag or retrieve them for up to 1 minute.

Any item placed within the bag is stored in its own extradimensional space, which is suffused by an antimagic field. The bag's mouth is 2 feet in diameter. It can hold up to 12 items, each weighing no more than 50 pounds, and weighs as much as the heaviest object stored within it.
When you retrieve an item from the bag, you always grab the item you intended.

entries.name: Cinnabar Compass
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the {@spell locate object} spell once without using a spell slot or spell components.

subclassFeature.name: Concussive Strike
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you score a critical hit on a melee attack against a creature, the target is stunned until the beginning of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Nothing to See Here
entries: At 10th level, with a blinding flash, you can overwrite the memories of those around you. You can cast the {@spell modify memory} spell once as an action, targeting 3 creatures within range, without using a spell slot or spell components. You must modify the memories of each creature affected by the spell in the same way.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Containment Dimension
entries: By 14th level, you can leverage a powerful quarantine procedure to keep others safe from dangerous artifacts. As an action, you can create a pocket dimension that is an exact duplicate of your surroundings at the moment of using this ability—complete with duplicates of all the structures and items therein—and transport creatures you choose within its area to the dimension. You decide the exact area of the duplicate dimension, as long as its total space fits within a 150-foot cube.

While in the pocket dimension, you can only affect and be affected by other creatures in that dimension. You can't see creatures and objects outside the pocket dimension. Objects taken from the pocket dimension vanish upon leaving it.

The pocket dimension lasts for 10 minutes, and ends early if it no longer contains living creatures or you use your action to end it. All creatures and objects inside the pocket dimension are deposited as near as possible to their corresponding locations in the real world when this effect ends.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Infernal Agent
entries: Just as noble investigators shield the world from sinister supernatural forces, infernal agents prowl the darkness enacting schemes to further supernatural goals. Though actual devils oversee many infernal agents, just as many count themselves as operatives of vampires, lycanthropes, ominous eldritch forces, and chaos itself. Their rationale and motives might vary, but all infernal agents seek to disrupt the status quo that other investigators protect, casting them in stark contrast and as bitter enemies.

subclassFeature.name: Sinister Emblem
entries: Starting when you choose this specialty at 3rd level, you manifest a sign of your benefactors, marking you as one of their number. Choose the Fist, Horns, or Shadow. Your mark's appearance reflects the nature of your benefactors: your Horns might be the devilish horns of a ram, or appear as vampire's fangs, or even eldritch, unblinking eyes.

entries.name: Fist
entries: One of your arms grows weighty with stone or scales, becoming a natural melee weapon with which you can make slam attacks. You have proficiency in your slam attack. On a hit, it deals {@dice 1d10} + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, and counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
Additionally, incoming attacks simply bounce off your arm. As a result, bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage that you take from nonmagical weapons is reduced by 3.

entries.name: Horns
entries: You grow a pair of horns, a direct connection to your benefactor's malevolent power. You can cast the following spells without using a spell slot or spell components: {@spell burning hands}, {@spell charm person}, {@spell hellish rebuke}, {@spell inflict wounds}.

This spell is cast at the level shown in the Ritual Level column of the Investigator table for your level. You can use this ability to cast a spell twice, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Shadow
entries: Your benefactors snip your shadow from your body, allowing it to move independently. Your shadow is a flat creature with 1 HP and 10 AC occupying a 5-foot square. On your turn, you can command your shadow to move up to your movement speed (no action required). You can use your bonus action to command your shadow to make a melee spell attack. This attack uses your spell attack bonus to hit and deals {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage on a hit.
Additionally, you can use your bonus action to command your shadow to take the Help action. It can take this action three times, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Your shadow vanishes if it enters an area of light created by a spell of 3rd level or higher or if it takes any damage. Once your shadow vanishes, you can resummon it at your heels as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've assembled a collection of cursed and baneful trinkets to help you achieve your malevolent goals. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Broken Ankh
entries: When you hit a celestial, elemental, or fiend with a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to activate this trinket, dealing an additional {@dice 2d10} necrotic or radiant damage (your choice) to the creature.

entries.name: Diabolical Bubble Gum
entries: As a bonus action, you can chew on this fiendish treat. Until the beginning of your next turn, whenever you take damage from a creature within 5 feet of you, you can deal {@dice 2d6} fire damage to that creature.

entries.name: Glass Demon's Eye
entries: You can use your bonus action to activate this trinket, allowing you to see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet for the next minute.

subclassFeature.name: Kick 'em While They're Down
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can capitalize on your foes' setbacks. Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@dice 2d6} damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Hide the Body
entries: By 10th level, you can flawlessly conceal the evidence of your misdeeds. Whenever you commit a murder, robbery, vandalism, or similar crime, you can spend 10 minutes hiding evidence at the scene. You can choose to remove all evidence connecting you to the crime, attempt to conceal the crime itself, or compose the crime scene to imply a specific conclusion.

subclassFeature.name: Master Plan
entries: By 14th level, you can set up the perfect crime by planning out its every detail. On your turn, you can choose a creature or object you can see within 60 feet and use your action to plan. The next attack roll or ability check you make to enact this plan has advantage. If you spend 1 minute planning a course of action, you have advantage on the next three attack rolls or ability checks you make enacting your plan.

subclassFeature.name: Kid Sleuth
entries: Though not all kid sleuths are literally juvenile, they are all amateur detectives with a knack for solving tricky crimes. Assisted by their ubiquitous talking animal sidekicks, kid sleuths will happily dive into the details of a grim crime scene to emerge with a handful of clues and a lead on the bad guy. These sleuths generally prefer to run away from trouble, rather than engage it head-on, since mysteries only get harder to solve with more dead bodies, but they are more than capable of defending themselves if backed into a corner.

subclassFeature.name: Talking Animal Sidekick
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain a kid sleuth's constant companion: a talking animal sidekick. Your animal sidekick can be any of the following animals: cat, crab, deer, goat, lizard, mastiff, owl, rat, raven, or weasel. This animal can speak Common and has Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma 10.

Your animal sidekick acts independently of you, but it always obeys your commands. In combat, it rolls its own initiative and acts on its own turn. An animal sidekick can't attack, but it can take other actions as normal. It can also use its action to sniff out clues, making an Investigation check with advantage to find any relevant information you missed.

If your animal sidekick drops to 0 hit points, it is knocked out instead of being killed. Your animal sidekick regains all its hit points when you finish a long rest, and makes its way back to you if it is ever separated from you.
If you drop to 0 hit points, your animal sidekick will do its best to protect you from harm.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've gotten your hands on a number of useful detective items for solving crimes, mysteries, and other hijinks. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Dooby Snack
entries: You produce a tasty meat-cracker snack as a bonus action. A creature which eats it on their turn (no action required) is bolstered for an hour. For this duration, the creature's hit point maximum and current hit points increase by 5.

entries.name: Magnifying Glass
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the {@spell clue|VSS} spell once without using a spell slot or spell components. Additionally, when you cast this spell, you can determine the type of each creature that leaves footprints or fingerprints.

entries.name: Monster Mask
entries: You can use your bonus action to activate this item, which reveals the true face of each creature you can see within 60 feet. A creature which is concealing its identity using a physical disguise or mask has this disguise magically tossed away, while a creature that is magically transformed or disguised has this magical effect suppressed until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Run Away
entries: Starting at 6th level, you prefer to flee from monsters and bad guys until you can pin down their methods, identities, and motives. Attacks of opportunity against you have disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Split Up, Gang
entries: By 10th level, you've discovered that you can cover a lot more ground if you split up. If you are further than 60 feet from your allies (excluding your animal sidekick), you can't be surprised. Furthermore, if you drop to 0 hit points while further than 60 feet from your allies, you drop to 1 hit point instead and can use your reaction to move up to your movement speed.
Once you use this feature to avoid dropping to 0 hit points, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Meddling Kids
entries: At 14th level, as a reaction when you see a hostile creature within 15 feet attack one of your allies, you can impose disadvantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Time Operative
entries: The end of all things has been foretold: soon, a horrific event will claim millions of lives. When such a disaster looms, a special pocketwatch is delivered from the future, promoting whoever receives it to an operative—one dedicated to preventing it. The pocketwatch is suffused with chronomantic energies, empowering anyone who holds it.

To avoid paradoxes, no clues can be sent backwards in time with the pocketwatch. Only a short message detailing the gravity of the situation can be sent. Therefore, it is inherent upon the time operative to solve the mystery and save the future from certain doom.

subclassFeature.name: Trinket
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you gain a powerful trinket which allows you to walk through time. Unlike other trinkets, you only regain uses of this ability once you take a long rest.

entries.name: Chronomancer's Pocketwatch
entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you activate the watch and magically return to the beginning of your turn. Any events which have happened over the course of your turn are undone, and you recover any actions and resources you expended over the course of your turn, except for your bonus action and the use of this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Temporal Reverb
entries: By 6th level, you can use other timelines as a backup when yours isn't optimal. Whenever you roll damage for a weapon attack, you can choose to reroll the damage, replacing the damage die with a die one step smaller (a d10 becomes a d8, a d8 becomes a d6, etc.). You can't use this ability to reroll a weapon with a d4 damage die.

subclassFeature.name: Echo of Yesterday
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can use your action to cast your senses backward in time, viewing your location as it was 24 hours prior. While viewing the past, you are blind and deaf with regard to your own senses. The past appears dreamlike and shadowy, but you are able to discern detail and hear conversation as normal, and any special senses you possess (such as darkvision) also work as normal.
While perceiving the past, you can look in any direction, but you cannot move or speak and are unable to sense your present surroundings. You can end this effect on your turn (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Steal Time
entries: Beginning at 14th level, when you use your Exploit Weakness feature against a creature, you can use your pocketwatch to steal moments from the target, which must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature freezes in place until the end of its next turn. For the duration, time doesn't pass for the creature: it is stunned and can't speak.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Antiquarian
entries: Festooned with magical trinkets from every corner of the globe, antiquarians have a tool for every occasion: silver arrowheads for lycanthropes, heartwood stakes for vampires, blessed relics for fiends, and so on. As they expand their collections from piles of trinkets to veritable museums, they become adept historians and arcanists, familiar with the story of every magic item their care, as well how to use them in dire situations.

subclassFeature.name: Artifact Historian
entries: Starting when you choose this specialty at 3rd level, you add the spell {@spell identify|PHB} to your grimoire and never require material components to cast it.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of magical trinkets to help you collect and unravel profound arcane secrets. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Hateful Arrowhead
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell scorching ray|PHB} or {@spell ray of enfeeblement|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Mirrored Prism
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell blur|PHB} or {@spell mirror image|PHB}, targeting yourself only, once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Razortooth Bandages
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell cure wounds|PHB} or {@spell inflict wounds|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components. This spell is cast as if using a spell slot of the level shown in the Ritual Level column of the Investigator table.

subclassFeature.name: Relics
entries: By 6th level, you've secured a handful of priceless relics with rare and delicate enchantments. You can use one of the following relics, and regain the ability to use this feature when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Antediluvian Dynamo
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell fireball|PHB} or {@spell lightning bolt|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Lich's Deathmask
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell counterspell|PHB} or {@spell dispel magic|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Mortal Coil
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell animate dead|PHB} or {@spell revivify|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components. Casting {@spell animate dead|PHB} using this trinket causes all undead servants created by previous castings of the spell to revert to lifeless corpses.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Item Collection
entries: Starting at 10th level, you maintain a vault of magic items, secured in an extradimensional space with an elaborate locking mechanism. You can produce one of these items, and change your selection each day when you finish a long rest.

The item is your choice of the following: a {@item carpet of flying|DMG}, a {@item cloak of the bat|DMG}, a {@item flame tongue|DMG}, {@item gauntlets of ogre power|DMG}, an {@item instant fortress|DMG}, a {@item ring of regeneration|DMG}, a {@item ring of telekinesis|DMG}, a {@item sun blade|DMG}, or a {@item wand of wonder}.

subclassFeature.name: Phylactery
entries: By 14th level, you've secured the crown jewel of your collection: a lich's phylactery. Though the original owner's soul has been expelled from this accursed artifact, it retains many of its unique properties.

This magic item is always attuned to you and doesn't count against your total number of attuned magic items. It has 5 charges, and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend one or more of these charges to use the following abilities:

entries.name: 1 Charge:
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell false life|PHB} as a second level spell without expending spell slots or spell components.

entries.name: 1 Charge:
entries: You can use your bonus action to regain an expended use of your Trinkets.

entries.name: 2 Charges:
entries: As a reaction when you drop to 0 hit points, you can drop to 1 hit point instead.

entries.name: 3 Charges:
entries: Make a melee spell attack against creature within your reach. On a hit, the target takes {@dice 5d6} necrotic damage and you regain hit points equal to the total amount of necrotic damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Archivist
entries: Though most investigators fill their grimoires with hard-won knowledge borne from encounters with the supernatural threats, some prefer to do bookkeeping instead. Such archivists accrue knowledge, not trinkets, by spending untold hours digging through disparate tomes of occult knowledge and compiling them into encyclopedic texts on the supernatural. Through their research, archivists become academic masters of the arcane, and priceless reservoirs of obscure knowledge.

subclassFeature.name: Thesis
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain access to certain spells associated with your thesis. Pick one of the following subject areas for your thesis: Corpus, Ignis, Mortis, or Regis. You gain a list of associated thesis spells. Once you gain access to a thesis spell, it is added to your grimoire. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the investigator ritual list, the spell is nonetheless an investigator spell for you. You can use your action and expend a use of your Rushed Incantation feature to cast one of your thesis spells.

subclassFeature.name: Trinket
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you've collected a powerful trinket to enhance your ritual magic:

entries.name: Archaic Scroll
entries: You can perform a ritual spell with your Rushed Incantation feature without expending one of the feature's uses.

subclassFeature.name: Erudite Spell
entries: By 6th level, you've mastered the difficult theory behind arcane propagation. When you cast a spell which forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw against the spell.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Encyclopedic Expertise
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can identify any arcane effect from memory. Whenever you witness a spell being cast or investigate a magical effect, you can always identify the spell that was cast, the magic item responsible, or the monster which produced the effect. However, this ability fails to identify spells, magic items, and monsters which are utterly unique or are otherwise not recorded in arcane texts.

subclassFeature.name: Eidetic Memory
entries: At 14th level, you can effortlessly duplicate spells you see in the world. You can copy any ritual spell you see being cast into your grimoire.

Moreover, you can precisely copy the exact motions of a spell's casting to duplicate its effect. When you see a spell of 5th level or lower being cast, you can use your action and spend 1 use of your Rushed Incantation feature as an action to cast the spell yourself without spending a spell slot. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action, must not have expensive spell components, and must have taken place within the last minute to use this ability.
Once you duplicate a spell in this way, you can't duplicate it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Detective
entries: Prowling at the edge of darkness, detectives chase down clues and pull on threads to unravel conspiracies which bring darkness into the world. Sometimes, this requires that you infiltrate a cult's secret meetings, other times it calls on you to reconstruct a man's last moments at a murder scene, but no matter what the mystery, you know that there is always an explanation.

subclassFeature.name: Investigator's Hunch
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you can unravel even the toughest of mysteries. If you spend at least 10 minutes combing through documents and arranging clues, you can make an Intelligence check with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of magical trinkets to aid you in tracking supernatural creatures and unraveling mysteries. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Glass Medalion
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell invisibility|PHB}, targeting yourself only, once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Fogstone Periapt
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell misty step|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Skeleton's Key
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell knock|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spellcomponents.

subclassFeature.name: Predictive Intuition
entries: By 6th level, you can turn your skills at reconstructing events towards anticipating the next likely moment of a fight. As a bonus action, you can examine the movements of a creature you can see within 30 feet. You can choose to add {@dice 1d4} to your next attack roll targeting that creature before the start of your next turn, or you can choose to subtract {@dice 1d4} from the creature's next attack roll against you before the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Interrogator's Instinct
entries: At 10th level, you are so accustomed to uncovering the truth that you can sense the intents behind someone's voice. You can tell if a creature that you can hear speaking is charmed, possessed, or otherwise enchanted to speak against their will, and you have advantage on any ability check you make to determine if you hear a lie.

subclassFeature.name: Power of Deduction
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can use your action to examine a creature you can see within 30 feet, taking note of innumerable details and making a lengthy string of logical deductions. For the next minute, you have advantage on Intelligence and Charisma checks you make to interact with this creature, and you have advantage on attack rolls against them.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and medium armor.

subclassFeature.name: Exterminator
entries: An exterminator suffers no monster to live. Trained in the art of slaying aberrations, fiends, and undead, you stand against evil where others falter, and draw your blade before others recognize a threat. Grand schemes and plots are less important than retribution against those monsters which stalk the night, and your thirst for such retribution is unquenchable. There is always another werewolf to be slain, another vampire to be staked, another demon to be banished; people rarely thank you, but you find satisfaction enough in your work.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of magical trinkets to aid you in the elimination of monsters and other fiendish threats. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Concecrated Whetstone
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell magic weapon|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Gilded Dragon Scale
entries: As a bonus action, you gain resistance to one damage type of your choice for a minute.

entries.name: Wyverntooth Necklace
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to activate this trinket, dealing an additional {@dice 2d6} acid damage to the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Monster Slayer
entries: Beginning at 6th level, when you use the Attack action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Silvered Edge
entries: Starting at 10th level, whenever you deal damage to a creature with a weapon attack, it can't regain hit points until the start of your next turn. Additionally, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, it can't be raised as an undead creature nor be returned to life for 7 days.

subclassFeature.name: Killer Instinct
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can use your Exploit Vulnerability feature twice on your turn, but can't use it against the same target more than once.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor

subclassFeature.name: Exorcist's Doctrines
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Religion} skill and add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.

subclassFeature.name: Inquisitor
entries: The church has long been the first line of defense against the tide of impending darkness. Yet, the clergy's stubborn devotion to righteousness impedes them where it counts: you must sometimes be willing to do evil to counter evil. That's where the inquisition comes in.

As a righteous inquisitor of the faith, you are tasked with rooting out heresy, exorcising demons, and stamping out any sign of the occult, and are offered clemency for any action you take in the defense of the greater good. You may investigate anyone or anything you deem to be in line with the forces of evil, for you alone are a holy blade in the dark, the arbiter of your church.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of magical trinkets to help you banish restless spirits and foul demonic forces. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Alabaster Balm
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell lesser restoration|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Hallowed Chalice
entries: As a bonus action, you can utter a prayer and produce a volume of {@item Holy Water (flask)|PHB|holy water} from this cup sufficient to fill one flask. After 24 hours, this {@item Holy Water (flask)|PHB|holy water} becomes ordinary water. You can use this trinket 5 times before its power is expended.

entries.name: Reliquary of Doubt
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell detect thoughts|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components. When casting the spell in this way, it can only detect thoughts which are associated with negative emotions, such as guilt, apprehension, regret, or melancholy.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: At 6th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@dice 1d6} radiant damage to the target.

subclassFeature.name: Rote Piety
entries: Starting at 10th level, you command a mastery of divine magic seldom seen among the clergy. You can use your Opportunistic Piety feature three times, and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Excommunication
entries: By 14th level, as an action, you can emblazon a creature you can see within 60 feet with a mark of religious condemnation. The target must succeed on your choice of a Constitution or Wisdom saving throw or be marked for the next minute. While marked, the creature can't regain hit points or have advantage on any attack roll or ability check. Additionally, whenever the creature takes an action, it takes {@dice 2d6} radiant damage as the mark burns with radiant light.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Fortelling
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you can receive glimpses of the future. When you finish a long rest, roll two d20s and record the number rolled. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature that you can see with this foretold roll. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

As an action, you can use a use of your Rushed Incantation ability to make an additional fortelling roll. You can have up to 3 foretold rolls at one time. Each foretelling roll can be used only once. When you finish a long rest, you lose any unused foretelling rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Medium
entries: As conduits between the living and dead, mediums offer an essential glimpse past the veil of mortality. Using their auguries, seances, and divinations, mediums can retrieve morsels of information from the afterlife to settle debts with the living or and assist ongoing investigations. This information, however cryptic, can point an interested party toward clues or evidence which only the deceased might be able to provide.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you've collected a number of objects which help you attune to the afterlife. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Dead Ringer
entries: As an action, you can ring this bell to cast the spell {@spell speak with dead|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components. When you cast this spell using this trinket, you can ask only one question of the corpse.

entries.name: Heptagonal Spectacles
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell see invisibility|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Lucent Mirror
entries: You can use your bonus action to activate this trinket, causing you to phase partially into the Ethereal Plane for up to 1 minute. For the duration, you can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but you take {@dice 1d10} force damage if you end your movement inside a creature or object. Additionally, you have resistance to all damage. This effect ends early after you take damage.

subclassFeature.name: Forewarning Presence
entries: By 6th level, the spirits of the dead guide your hand when you would falter. You can reroll an attack roll or ability check you make, and must use the new roll.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Whispers From Beyond
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can open your perceptions to receive a hint from beyond the pale. The GM answers with a one-word hint pertaining to your best course of action, a fruitful line of inquiry, or some other useful direction.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Third Eye
entries: By 14th level, your connection to the afterlife allows you to see all. You can use your bonus action to cast the spell true seeing without using a spell slot or spell components.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Occultist
entries: Vampires, demons, lycanthropes, and aberrations all have one thing in common: they are all magical threats, best combated through magical means. To meet these foes on a level playing field, occultists indulge in arcana, filling their grimoires with magical secrets and mastering a handful of spells. Occultists are the most likely investigators to cavort with warlocks, borrow tricks from wizards and magicians, and dabble in dark magic to defeat their foes.

subclassFeature.name: Pact Magic
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you augment your investigative skills with complex magicks.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}. You learn an additional warlock cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Occultist Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have. The table also shows what the level of those slots is; all of your spell slots are the same level.
To cast one of your warlock spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
entries: At 3rd level, you know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}.

The Spells Known column of the Occultist table shows when you learn more warlock spells of your choice of 1st level and higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class and order, you can choose one of the warlock spells you know and replace is with another spell from the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your warlock spells, so you use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. Use your investigator spell save DC and spell attack bonus for your warlock spells.

subclassFeature.name: Trinket
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you've acquired a trinket to assist in your collection of eldritch lore:

entries.name: Rune Keeper Eyeglass
entries: As a bonus action, for the next hour, you can read all writing.

subclassFeature.name: Eldritch Ruin
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can use your magical cunning to rip your foes asunder. You can cast a cantrip as a bonus action instead of dealing additional damage with your Exploit Vulnerability feature.

subclassFeature.name: Eyes of Another World
entries: Starting at 10th level, supernatural creatures can no longer escape your detection. You can see creatures and objects that are invisible or ethereal, and you can see the original form of shapechangers or creatures whose form has been altered by magic. Additionally, you can immediately detect if a creature you can see is possessed.

subclassFeature.name: Maleficium
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you use your Exploit Weakness feature against a creature, you can cast the spell bestow curse on that creature as a bonus action, without using a spell slot or spell components; the targeted creature has disadvantage on its saving throw against this spell.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bravado
entries: Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, your knowing smile and infectious confidence inspires trust in most people you meet, even when you're not telling the truth. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill and add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.

subclassFeature.name: Spy
entries: Infiltration, disguise, and lying through their teeth: these are the principle skills of a spy. A talented spy rarely ever needs to draw a dagger to silence someone, for their honeyed words and agreeable disposition are all that is needed to draw out someone's secrets. Of course, when they do strike, it is decisively and without warning. Such skills are invaluable for governments and organizations of all varieties, for stealing confidential information, sabotage, monitoring persons of interest, and everything in between.

subclassFeature.name: Trinkets
entries: By 3rd level, you have a collection of trinkets which aid your espionage. You can use the following trinkets:

entries.name: Glass Dust
entries: As a bonus action, you can throw this dust in the air, becoming invisible until the beginning of your next turn. This effect ends early if you take damage.

entries.name: Horn-Rimmed Glasses
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell disguise self|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

entries.name: Martini Glass
entries: As a bonus action, you can cast the spell {@spell charm person|PHB} once without using a spell slot or spell components.

subclassFeature.name: Cloak and Dagger
entries: By 6th level, you always endeavor to kill your target in one strike. When you make an attack roll against a creature that is surprised or hasn't taken a turn in combat yet, you can maximize your weapon's damage die.

subclassFeature.name: Shaken Not Stirred
entries: Starting at 10th level, your roguish smile grants you incredible leeway. Whenever you fail a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) or Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you can reroll the check, and must use the results of the new roll.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Body Double
entries: By 14th level, you can rapidly exchange identities with a corpse. When you reduce a humanoid creature to 0 hit points, you can assume the creature's appearance, as per the disguise self spell. The creature's body, as well as its blood, clothing, and other physical evidence of its death, become invisible for 8 hours. You can also use this ability as an action to touch the corpse of a humanoid creature which has been dead no longer than 1 day, assuming its appearance and causing the body to become invisible.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Anonymity
entries: Like other martyrs, you were chosen to accomplish great things and die for a noble cause. However, you were not destined for glory; no one will ever know your name. Martyrs chosen for the Burden of Anonymity work in the shadows to influence the world for the greater good, and will inevitably die in the shadows, unknown to all.

Your divinely-appointed task might be secret, such as assassination or infiltration, and even your role as a martyr might be incognito, even to your allies. Anonymous martyrs might disguise themselves as paladins, mercenaries, or rogues with hidden agendas and abundant conspiracies.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Fade from View
entries: Also at 1st level, you can slip out of sight the moment things heat up. When you roll initiative and aren't surprised, you can become invisible, as per the {@spell invisibility} spell, until the end of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Innate Subtlety
entries: Starting at 1st level, you have a gift for secrecy and treachery alike. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill and with {@item thieves' tools|PHB}. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.
You also learn the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

subclassFeature.name: Silent Torment
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a creature that can't see you or is surprised, the target is magically silenced until the start of your next turn, rendering it unable to speak or perform the verbal components of spells. Additionally, you don't lose any hit points from using the Torment ability.

subclassFeature.name: Instant Alias
entries: Beginning at 14th level, you can magically claim the identity of any person you slay. Whenever you reduce a Humanoid to 0 hit points with a melee attack, you can instantly cause any of the following effects:

items: You can instantly assume the creature's appearance, as per the {@spell disguise self} spell for 8 hours.
You can unerringly mimic the creature's speech.
You learn one of the creature's Bonds or Flaws.
The creature's corpse becomes invisible for 1 hour.

subclassFeature.name: False Martyrdom
entries: At 18th level, as a reaction when you take damage or use the Undying Conviction feature, you can convincingly fake your death. You teleport up to a known location within 10 miles of you at the same time that a duplicate corpse appears to take your place. The corpse is indistinguishable from you and wears nonmagical copies of your clothing and equipment. You can choose for the corpse to realistically bleed, mutter incoherently, and breathe shallowly for up to a minute. The corpse is unaffected by spells and magical effects. The corpse and its clothing and equipment vanish after 10 days, or when you dismiss it as an action on your turn or use this feature again.

Furthermore, if you covertly notify the GM, you can also convince your allies of your death. You can roll death saving throws as normal, ignoring successes, temporarily prepare a new character sheet, and so on.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Ascension
entries: The gods have set an arduous path for your life, replete with labors, challenges, and trials designed to test your morality and resolve. You likely won't succeed. However, if you best the gods' trials and die in marvelous glory, you will ascend to the afterlife as a demigod.

Perhaps the gods envision you as a replacement for a recently-deceased god, or perhaps they raise dozens of demigods to do their bidding across the planes of existence. Regardless of your status, immortality after death awaits.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

rows: {@spell beacon of hope}, {@spell protection from energy}

subclassFeature.name: Herculean Surge
entries: Also at 1st level, you can channel the might and agility of the gods. As a bonus action, you can gain a bonus to your Strength and Dexterity scores for 1 minute. This bonus is +4 if the ability score is 14 or lower, or +2 otherwise, up to a maximum of 22.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Labors of Ascension
entries: Starting at 1st level, the GM chooses five to twelve labors—critical challenges with divine implications—for you to complete in your lifetime, but doesn't need to disclose what the labors entail. You have advantage on any ability check you make that you know will advance or help complete one of your labors. Whenever you complete one of your labors, your current and maximum hit points increases by 5.

subclassFeature.name: Bloody Reprieve
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a hostile creature and you reduce the target to 0 hit points, you don't lose any hit points from using the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Relics of Legend
entries: By 14th level, your legendary deeds have imbued your most iconic equipment with supernatural blessings. You choose three pieces of equipment to be your Aegis, Oracle, and Xiphos, detailed below. These items might have been at your side for years, or they might have been used to accomplish one of your labors, or may even be trophies taken from your mightiest foes.
Once you use your Aegis, Oracle, or Xiphos, you can't use that relic again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Aegis
entries: Your Aegis is a shield, an article of clothing, or a pelt that grants you divine protection. You can use your Aegis to cast the spell {@spell shield} without using hit points or counting against your total number of martyr spells per day.

entries.name: Oracle
entries: Your Oracle is a stone, gemstone, or another accessory that allows you to communicate with the gods. You can use your Oracle to cast the spell {@spell commune} without using hit points or counting against your total number of martyr spells per day.

entries.name: Xiphos
entries: Your Xiphos is a sword, spear, or other melee weapon that you have wielded in combat. You can use your Xiphos when you score a critical hit with this relic or reduce a creature to 0 hit points with it, you can move up to 15 feet and make another melee weapon attack as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Demigod Ascendant
entries: At 18th level, the power of demigods flows through your veins in anticipation of your final ascension. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Improved Herculean Surge
entries: Your Herculean Surge improves. When you use this feature, your Strength and Dexterity scores gain a +4 bonus, up to a maximum of 24, for 1 minute. You regain the use of your Herculean Surge whenever you roll initiative.

entries.name: Legendary Resistance
entries: If you fail a saving throw, you can use your reaction to succeed instead. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Calamity
entries: A grim task awaits those shouldering the Burden of Calamity. These martyrs spark wars, conjure floods, raise plagues of locusts, call down fire and brimstone, and set apocalypses in motion. As benevolent as the gods might seem, their mercy is tempered with disasters such as these—events that the gods deem necessary to cleanse unjust societies or wipe the slate clean for new advancements. Martyrs of Calamity are therefore harbingers of doom, the first omen of terrible things to follow.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

entries.name: Harbinger
entries: Starting at 1st level, you are an incorrigible ne'er-do-well. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Intimidation} skill and with a set of {@item artisan's tools|phb} of your choice. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.
You also learn the {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

entries.name: Aura of Calamity
entries: Starting at 1st level, as an action, you can summon an elemental storm around you in a 10-foot-radius aura. When you use this feature, choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, or thunder damage for the aura and designate any number of creatures you can see to be unaffected by it.

When an affected creature enters the aura for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone and takes {@dice 1d8} damage of the type you chose. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't knocked prone.
The aura lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated or die, or you dismiss it early on your turn (no action required).

At higher levels, you can empower your aura with your life force. At 5th level, you can choose to lose 5 hit points when you use this feature to increase the aura's damage to {@dice 2d8}. At 11th level, you can lose 10 hit points to increase the aura's damage to {@dice 3d8}, and at 17th level, you can lose 15 hit points to increase the aura's damage to {@dice 4d8}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bloody Reprieve
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a hostile creature and you reduce the target to 0 hit points, you don't lose any hit points from using the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Denounce the Wicked
entries: By 14th level, your proclamations and condemnations carry divine weight. When you finish a long rest, you can speak aloud the name of a specific creature known to you (or a unique pseudonym, title, or nickname) and denounce that creature. Until you finish a long rest, whenever you see the denounced creature make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that roll.

subclassFeature.name: Doom Foretold
entries: At 18th level, you can call upon all of the gods' malice to create a sweeping disaster. You can cast the spell {@spell control weather}, {@spell earthquake}, {@spell fire storm}, or {@spell storm of vengeance} without using hit points or counting against your total number of martyr spells per day.
Once you use this feature to cast one of these spells, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Fame
entries: You were destined to see your name in lights, to be the talk of the town, to be a superstar. Unfortunately, the gods also chose you to go out in a blaze of glory. The Burden of Fame entails a life of renown matched only by royalty, coupled with incredible duties undertaken in the public eye. Your flame burns brightly for half as long, but when your journey is complete, nobody will forget your name.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Celebrity
entries: Starting at 1st level, your name rings familiar to people you meet. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill and with one {@item musical instrument|PHB} of your choice. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.
You also learn the {@spell dancing lights} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

subclassFeature.name: Famous Autograph
entries: By 1st level, your reputation precedes you. You can use a bonus action to attempt to charm a creature that can hear or see you within 60 feet, which must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier). A creature with an Intelligence score of 3 or less automatically succeeds on this saving throw.
For any other creature, roll a {@dice d20}. If you roll lower than your martyr level, the creature has already heard of you and automatically fails its saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature is {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute or until your companions do anything harmful to it. The target regards you as a celebrity and is starstruck by your presence. If you use your action to sign your autograph for the charmed creature, the duration of this effect extends to 10 minutes.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sacrosanct Spell
entries: At 6th level, when you cast one of your Burden Spells or a spell that has the sole effect of causing a creature to regain hit points, you lose no hit points from casting the spell if you cast it at its lowest level. Casting the spell still counts against your total number of spell uses.

subclassFeature.name: Crowd Suggestion
entries: By 14th level, your renown drives crowds to your side. You can cast the spell {@spell mass suggestion} without using hit points or counting against your total number of martyr spells per day. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

You can also stretch the spell's magic to influence more targets with less specificity. You can target up to 100 creatures within range and deliver a one-or two-word suggestion. Each target or group of targets (as decided by the GM) must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or collectively follow the suggestion.
You can't specify conditions that will trigger a special activity during the duration or suggest any activity that will be harmful to one or more members of the group.

Some typical commands and their effects follow. You might issue a command other than one described here. If you do so, the GM determines how targets behave. If a target can't follow your command, the spell ends for it.

entries.name: Catch Me!
entries: A target helps to lift and move you above its head, passing you to a creature adjacent to it in the direction you specify.

entries.name: Disperse!
entries: A target spends its turn moving until it is at least 10 feet away from other creatures.

entries.name: Get Them!
entries: A target spends its turn moving toward a creature you specify and attempting to grapple it, if able.

entries.name: Hide Me!
entries: A target spends its turn moving toward you by the shortest and most direct route, attempting to conceal you in any way it deems appropriate.

entries.name: Jump!
entries: A target must use all its movement to jump up and down without leaving its space and has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws and attack rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Superstar Sanctitude
entries: Beginning at 18th level, every Humanoid you meet recognizes you as the most beloved and iconic personality of your age.
Additionally, you can cast the spell {@spell sanctuary} at will, targeting yourself, without using hit points or counting against your total number of martyr spells per day.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Levity
entries: When bacchanal and trickster deities believe that the mortal realm has gotten too self-serious, they call upon a Martyr of Levity to lighten the mood. These impish martyrs are given license to joke, prank, and romp about the world with extraordinarily vague directives. Perhaps they have been commanded to pull a prank or tell a joke so ludicrous that history remembers it forever. Or perhaps the martyr is themselves the butt of a larger joke, a walking parable about fate's blunders. Sadly, the martyr's death usually serves as a punchline.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

rows: {@spell disguise self}, {@spell tasha's hideous laughter||hideous laughter}

subclassFeature.name: Ne'er-Do-Well
entries: Starting at 1st level, you are an incorrigible ne'er-do-well. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill and with one {@item gaming set|PHB} of your choice. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.
You also learn the {@spell prestidigitation} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

subclassFeature.name: Pie in the Face
entries: At 1st level, you can use a bonus action to throw a pie at a creature you can see within 30 feet, which must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier) or be {@condition blinded} until the start of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sacrosanct Spell
entries: At 6th level, when you cast one of your Burden Spells or a spell that has the sole effect of causing a creature to regain hit points, you lose no hit points from casting the spell if you cast it at its lowest level. Casting the spell still counts against your total number of spell uses.

subclassFeature.name: Just a Prank
entries: By 14th level, your puckish nature helps you escape consequences for your actions. As a reaction when one or more creatures you can see become hostile toward you, you can magically suppress their hostility by declaring it was an elaborate prank. Each hostile creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC.

On a failed save, a target becomes indifferent about you, and you have advantage on the next Charisma check you make directed at it within the next hour. This indifference ends if the target is attacked or harmed by a spell or if it witnesses any of its friends being harmed.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Zany Misdirection
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can use your reaction when you are targeted by an attack to change the target of that attack to a creature within 5 feet of you that is also within the attack's range or reach. If there is no such creature within 5 feet of you, the attack has disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Odyssey
entries: The gods have tasked you with exploring the far reaches of the world, to venture where no soul has gone before, and bring back the discoveries waiting to be found there. You might be tasked to map an undiscovered continent, discover a strait between distant cities, or sail the edge of the oceans, but peril and adventure regardless await. You are unlikely to return from your journey, but your expedition will not have been in vain as long as your odyssey is remembered.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Pilgrim's Gate
entries: Also at 1st level, you can use your bonus action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. You can also bring one willing creature of your size or smaller who is carrying gear up to its carrying capacity. The creature must be within 5 feet of you when you use this feature.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Worldly Experience
entries: When you choose this burden at 1st level, your walking speed increases by 10 feet.
Additionally, you gain proficiency in cartographer's tools and learn one language of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Bloody Reprieve
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a hostile creature and you reduce the target to 0 hit points, you don't lose any hit points from using the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Zephyr's Stride
entries: At 14th level, whenever you end your turn 30 feet or further away from where you began your turn, you are enveloped by a blur of speed. Attacks against you have disadvantage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Chosen of the Argonauts
entries: At 18th level, the gods hasten you and your companions onward to your inevitable end. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can choose six willing creatures to gain a blessing of swiftness, which lasts until the creature finishes a long rest. Each creature blessed this way gains a climbing speed and a swimming speed equal to its walking speed, and can use its bonus action to move 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting at 1st level, you gain proficiency in {@filter heavy armor|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=heavy armor}.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Atonement
entries: For all the wrong you have done in your life, the gods have graced you with an undeserved second chance. Your burden is to atone, to undo all the evils you have done in your life, and to seek redemption before the gods. How you accomplish this is your choice, so long as you remove any dark marks you leave behind. Ultimately, your final martyrdom might be a grand act, averting a great disaster or banishing a force of evil, or it might be as humble as redirecting the life of an evil person and causing them to seek redemption, passing the torch to a new martyr of atonement.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Self-Sacrifice
entries: Also at 1st level, you can dive in the way of an attack. When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to change the target of the attack to yourself instead.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Blooded Reprieve
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a hostile creature and you reduce the target to 0 hit points, you lose no hit points from using the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Draw Aggression
entries: Beginning at 14th level, as a bonus action on your turn, you can invoke the ire of your foes to protect your allies. Until the start of your next turn, each hostile creature you choose within 5 feet of you that can see you has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and advantage on attack rolls against you.

subclassFeature.name: Sin Eater
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can use your action to transfer negative effects from any number of willing creatures within 60 feet to yourself. You can transfer one of the following effects from each creature:

items: One level of {@condition exhaustion}
One disease or poison
One curse, including the target's attunement to a cursed magic item
Any reduction to one of the target's ability scores
One effect reducing the target's hit point maximum
• The {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition petrified}, or {@condition poisoned} condition

entries: The effect ends for the creature and targets you as if you were the original target, with the effect's original duration. You can transfer multiple negative effects of the same type to yourself, affecting you only once each time you use this ability.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Discord
entries: Martyrs of Discord are chosen at random to sow chaos and stoke evil wherever they travel. They light things on fire, commit petty crimes, and even murder when the fancy strikes. Given to their own predilections, martyrs of discord are unified only by their charge to disrupt the status quo as much as possible.

No one is certain why the gods make martyrs of Discord in the first place. Perhaps it is at the whim of one capricious or evil god, or perhaps the gods desire a balance between chaos and law, and must sometimes stoke the fire to restore this balance.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

rows: {@spell tasha's hideous laughter||hideous laughter}, {@spell inflict wounds}

subclassFeature.name: Cantrips
entries: Starting at 1st level, your divine mission grants you a sliver of divine magic. You learn the {@spell shocking grasp} and {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrips, and one other cantrip of your choice from the {@filter cleric spell list|spells|class=cleric}. You do not lose hit points for casting these spells. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

subclassFeature.name: Havoc!
entries: Also at 1st level, you can stoke the flames of chaos with your sword. When you hit a hostile creature with a melee weapon attack, you can roll on the table below to trigger an additional effect.
Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

rows: You trade places with the target.
Lightning strikes you and the target, dealing {@dice 3d6} lightning damage to each of you.
The target must vocalize their innermost thoughts for the next minute.
You cast {@spell darkness}, centered on the target.
You become invisible until the end of your next turn, as per the spell {@spell invisibility}.

The target bursts into flames, taking {@dice 1d4} fire damage. The target continues to take {@dice 1d4} fire damage at the beginning of their turns until a creature extinguishes the flames as an action.
The target's skin transforms into a brittle glass until the end of its next turn, reducing its AC by 3.
Something explodes in the distance, dealing {@dice 8d6} fire damage to any creature within a 20-foot radius of the exploding object.
Roll on this table twice and apply both results. If you get this result on one or both secondary rolls, ignore it.

subclassFeature.name: Blooded Reprieve
entries: Starting at 6th level, whenever you use your {@classFeature Torment|Martyr|VSS|3} feature against a hostile creature and you reduce the target to 0 hit points, you lose no hit points from using the ability.

subclassFeature.name: Coin of Chaos
entries: At 14th level, you have a coin blessed with the tides of chaos. Whenever you or a creature you can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can {@coinflip flip a coin|Coin of Chaos|Heads (+4)|Tails (+1)} to add a bonus to the roll. The bonus equals +4 on a heads or +1 on a tails. Once you use the coin, the coin goes to the GM, who can use it to enhance any roll made by an NPC or monster. Once the GM has used the coin, it passes back to you, and so on. When you finish a long rest, you regain the coin, whether or not the GM has used it.

subclassFeature.name: Pandemonium
entries: At 18th level, you can use your Havoc! ability whenever you make an attack roll or cast a spell targeting a hostile creature, and do not need to rest between uses.

subclassFeature.name: Balm
entries: Also at 1st level, you can use a bonus action to restore 1 hit point to a creature within 60 feet or remove the {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition poisoned} condition from a willing creature you touch. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Mercy
entries: When the suffering of the world grows too heavy, and the cries of the anguished grow too loud, the gods deliver a martyr to ease the strife and bring peace to the masses. This burden, however, has no end. A martyr may spend all their days healing the sick and driving demons out of the possessed, but they will scarcely impact the world at large. Indeed, the onus upon them is not to mend every wound or comfort every widow, but to provide hope in the darkest of times that the gods have not forsaken their people, that peace will soon reign, and that light will be victorious.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Cantrips
entries: Starting at 1st level, your divine mission grants you a sliver of divine magic. You learn the {@spell spare the dying} and {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrips, and one other cantrip of your choice from the {@filter cleric spell list|spells|class=cleric}. You do not lose hit points for casting these spells. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

subclassFeature.name: Sacrosanct Spell
entries: At 6th level, when you cast a spell with a casting time of 1 minute or longer, or a spell that has the sole effect of restoring hit points, you lose no hit points from casting the spell if you cast it at its lowest level. Casting the spell still counts against your total number of spell uses.

subclassFeature.name: Shared Respite
entries: Beginning at 14th level, when you use your {@classFeature Divine Healing|Martyr|VSS|3} ability to expend Hit Dice and regain hit points, one willing creature you choose within 60 feet can also regain hit points equal to the highest number rolled on a hit die + your Constitution modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Anointed Healer
entries: By 18th level, your status as a divine healer is enshrined in sainthood. Whenever you restore hit points to a creature, you can add your martyr level to the number of regained hit points. Any creature can benefit from this additional healing once, regaining the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Burden of Rebirth
entries: The gods have seen a great cataclysm on the horizon and tasked their martyr with protecting the world from catastrophe. The exact nature of the coming End may vary: sometimes the awakening or arrival of an evil entity, sometimes the discovery and abuse of a powerful artifact, and sometimes a singularly destructive event that endangers the gods themselves, but the martyr must be dedicated to give their lives to stop it. To this end, the gods imbue their martyr with ancient and powerful spells, tools once gifted only to prophets.

When the coming cataclysm threatens the world, a martyr would be ill-served to ignore their call, for the cataclysm imperils them directly—as well as the lives of their family and companions. The life of a martyr is always forfeit, but the rest of the world can yet be saved.

entries.name: Burden Spells
entries: You gain burden spells at the martyr levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Cantrips
entries: Starting at 1st level, your divine mission grants you a sliver of divine magic. You learn the {@spell druidcraft} and {@spell shillelagh} cantrips, and one other cantrip of your choice from the {@filter druid spell list|spells|class=druid}. You do not lose hit points for casting these spells. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

subclassFeature.name: Friend of The Forest
entries: Also at 1st level, you can cast the {@spell speak with animals} spell without losing hit points to cast it. When you do so, you have advantage on Charisma ability checks made against beasts for the duration of the spell.
Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sacrosanct Spell
